name: AI-Powered-OSINT-Company-Monitor

documentation.readme: An automated OSINT platform that monitors companies through public RSS feeds and web sources, extracts named entities (companies, products, technologies, people…), and alerts on mentions of watched companies.
usage_sections: GLiNER zero-shot NER refines entity types (150 ms)
Combined result: 50 ms with custom labels (Company, Product, Technology, Person, Financial Amount, Location)
Git Bash / WSL / Linux/macOS (for shell scripts)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

examples: uv run python -m spacy download en_core_web_sm
uv run python -m spacy download fr_core_news_sm

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with Axelaxl/exploiting-phone (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with thewhiteh4t/seeker (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with n3twork5/xharvester (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web] in the OSINT category. Fits when the engagement needs: AI-Powered OSINT Company Monitor.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: An automated OSINT platform that monitors companies through public RSS feeds and web sources, extracts named entities (companies, products, technologies, people…), and alerts on mentions of watched companies.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone <repo-url>. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `docker compose up -d`. Further invocations from upstream: `uv run python -m spacy download en_core_web_sm`; `uv run python -m spacy download fr_core_news_sm`.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need OSINT, use AI-Powered-OSINT-Company-Monitor because AI-Powered OSINT Company Monitor.
When the engagement is in the recon phase, use AI-Powered-OSINT-Company-Monitor as a cataloged OSINT tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `docker compose up -d` as documented upstream for AI-Powered-OSINT-Company-Monitor.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `uv run python -m spacy download en_core_web_sm` as documented upstream for AI-Powered-OSINT-Company-Monitor.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `uv run python -m spacy download fr_core_news_sm` as documented upstream for AI-Powered-OSINT-Company-Monitor.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `uv run uvicorn backend.main:app --host $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST --port 8000` as documented upstream for AI-Powered-OSINT-Company-Monitor.
When chaining OSINT tools, pair AI-Powered-OSINT-Company-Monitor with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `AI-Powered-OSINT-Company-Monitor --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: uv run python -m spacy download en_core_web_sm
uv run python -m spacy download fr_core_news_sm
